message:
fix(transport): use u64 sequence space in SymbolReorderer

ObjectReorderState previously tracked sequences as u32 with wrapping
arithmetic via seq_after(). This was fragile: the signed-difference
test for wrap-around is correct but easy to misuse, and BTreeMap<u32>
with wrapping keys has undefined ordering near the wrap point.

The fix unwraps u32 wire sequences into a monotonic u64 space:
- next_expected starts at 1<<32 (avoiding underflow on late duplicates)
- Incoming u32 sequences are compared via wrapping subtraction against
  the low 32 bits of next_expected, yielding a signed offset
- The offset is applied to next_expected to get the unwrapped u64 key
- BTreeMap<u64> has correct natural ordering, no wrap-around ambiguity

The seq_after() helper is removed entirely — all ordering decisions
now use plain integer comparison on the unwrapped space.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
